mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, designed for high throughput and low resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring efficient caching and real-time data processing. Key trade-offs include compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached APIs versus the need for a new operational model.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://charts.dragonflydb.io

```

> This is the official repository for Dragonfly Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This command installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dragonfly to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb_v1alpha1_dragonfly_crd.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying Dragonfly.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This command deploys Dragonfly into your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --version 1.37.0 --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace

```

> This installs Dragonfly in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Dragonfly Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Dragonfly instance to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.io/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: example-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.io/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: example-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dragonfly pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Look for 'Running' status in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dragonfly from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> This command will remove the Dragonfly deployment.
> This will delete the namespace and any orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Dragonfly to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --version 1.37.0 --namespace dragonfly

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the node you drained earlier.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the Dragonfly pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dragonfly' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redis'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly instance cannot connect to the Redis backend.

**Fix:**
Verify your Redis configuration and ensure it is accessible from the Dragonfly pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for Dragonfly.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in your Dragonfly deployment or scale your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct RoleBindings or ClusterRoleBindings.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new caching algorithms and improved memory management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated API endpoints for legacy configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.36.0, ensure to update your configuration files to match the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods in 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
